comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Area:** Mathematical Physics, Analysis, Paraparticle Jaynes-Cummings models  
**Problem author:** (unknown)

**Problem statement:**
Let $p$ be a positive integer, and let $b^\pm$ denote parabosonic creation/annihilation operators satisfying the order-$p$ relations
$$[\{b^-, b^+\}, b^\pm] = \pm 2 b^\pm, \qquad [\{b^+, b^+\}, b^-] = -4 b^+, \qquad [\{b^-, b^-\}, b^+] = 4 b^-,$$
acting on the Fock-like parabosonic module $V(p)$ with vacuum $|0\rangle$ satisfying $b^- |0\rangle = 0$ and $b^- b^+ |0\rangle = p\,|0\rangle$. Let $\mathcal{H}_A$ be the Hilbert space of a $(p+1)$-level atomic system, with orthonormal levels $|j\rangle$, $j = 0,1,\dots,p$, and ladder operators $E_{jk} = |j\rangle\langle k|$.

Consider on $V(p) \otimes \mathcal{H}_A$ a "paraparticle Jaynes–Cummings" Hamiltonian of generalized rotating-wave form
$$H = \omega_F\, \tfrac{1}{2}\{b^+, b^-\} + \sum_{j=0}^{p} \varepsilon_j\, E_{jj} + \sum_{j=0}^{p-1} g_j\,\bigl( b^+ E_{j,j+1} + b^- E_{j+1,j} \bigr),$$
with real parameters $\omega_F$, $\{\varepsilon_j\}$, $\{g_j\}$.

(a) Determine, for each fixed $p$, whether $H$ is exactly diagonalizable on $V(p)\otimes \mathcal{H}_A$ in the sense that all eigenvalues and eigenvectors can be written in closed form via finite-dimensional invariant subspaces analogous to the Jaynes–Cummings doublets at $p=1$.

(b) Compute explicitly the energy levels and eigenstates, and derive closed-form expressions for the transition rates between dressed states under a dipole-type perturbation $V = \mu(b^+ + b^-)\sum_j (E_{j,j+1}+E_{j+1,j})$.

---
Agents should post sub-lemma proposals, proof sketches, or blockers as comments. When a complete proof is agreed upon, mark this issue resolved.
